content.problemStatement:
Write a program to calculate the total marks obtained in three subjects where the marks may include decimal values (`85.5`, `90.0` & `88.3`). Use the addition (`+`) operator and print the total marks with one decimal place.

**Hint**: Use %.1f to print a float value with 1 digit after the decimal point. (We will learn more about such formatting in the float topic.)
constraints: Each mark is given with at most one decimal place
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines, each containing a decimal number (0.0 to 100.0) with at most one digit after the decimal point.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line in the format: Total Marks = X where X is the sum of the three marks printed with exactly one digit after the decimal point.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The three marks are 85.5, 90.0 and 88.3. Their sum is 263.8, printed with one decimal place.
Adding the three equal marks 50.0 each gives 150.0.

solution.approach: The problem requires calculating the sum of three decimal numbers and printing the result with one digit after the decimal point. This can be achieved by using the addition operator and formatting the output to display one decimal place.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; scanf("%d",&T); for(int i=0;i<T;++i){double m1,m2,m3; scanf("%lf %lf %lf",&m1,&m2,&m3); double total=m1+m2+m3; printf("Total Marks = %.1f\n",total);} return 0;}
code.Python3:
import sys
T=int(sys.stdin.readline().strip())
for _ in range(T):
    m1=float(sys.stdin.readline().strip())
    m2=float(sys.stdin.readline().strip())
    m3=float(sys.stdin.readline().strip())
    total=m1+m2+m3
    print(f"Total Marks = {total:.1f}")
code.JavaScript: const lines = require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf-8').trim().split('\n'); let i=0; const T=parseInt(lines[i++],10); let res=[]; for(let i2=0;i2<T;++i2){ let m1=parseFloat(lines[i++]); let m2=parseFloat(lines[i++]); let m3=parseFloat(lines[i++]); let total=m1+m2+m3; res.push(`Total Marks = ${total.toFixed(1)}`);} console.log(res.join('\n'));
